title: Cherry-Pick, Replay, Patch Equivalence, and Backports · History Workshop
summary: A pairing session predicts each repository-state transition before building the first multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record for cherry-pick, replay, patch equivalence, and backports. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select and order commits for cherry-pick using explicit revision traversal while distinguishing argument order from revision-walk order.
Handle merge mainline, empty, redundant, no-commit, signoff, and conflict continuation choices with release-policy evidence.
Use patch-id, cherry marks, range-diff, and tree comparisons to detect likely equivalent changes while naming heuristic limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Case G32 is a workshop in a disposable repository. one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice. The learner must predict minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target, run git cherry -v release security-fix, explain the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work, test this changed condition: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior, and preserve this recovery boundary: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select and order commits for cherry-pick using explicit revision traversal while distinguishing argument order from revision-walk order.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-backport-verification-1klf5y" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Backport Verification into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-backport-verification-1klf5y
# scenario-key: trace-1klf5y probe-y5flk1 boundary-lf5y1k owner-1klf5yx fault-1klf5yz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1k candidate=b00b15y
# hypothesis: one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice
git cherry -v release security-fix
# expected: minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target
# observation: the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work
# changed-case: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior
# after-ref: main=a11ce1k candidate=c0ffeey
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations
# transfer-limit: patch equivalence is heuristic and each supported runtime needs changed-platform tests
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Gita Backport Verification structural check is only one gate. multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Gita Backport Verification condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Gita Backport Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G41 is a workshop in a disposable repository. one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice. The learner must predict minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target, run git cherry -v release security-fix, explain the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work, test this changed condition: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior, and preserve this recovery boundary: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations. Competency probe: Verify source provenance, target applicability, conflicts, changed-platform behavior, release notes, signatures, and rollback for each supported line.
steps.why: Retrieving Gita Backport Verification through the concrete multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify source provenance, target applicability, conflicts, changed-platform behavior, release notes, signatures, and rollback for each supported line.

hints: Name the Gita Backport Verification precondition or invariant that the workshop case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G41 is a workshop in a disposable repository. one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice. The learner must predict minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target, run git cherry -v release security-fix, explain the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work, test this changed condition: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior, and preserve this recovery boundary: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations. Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A clean cherry-pick means the fix is complete on an older dependency and runtime stack.
